essence: Installs windows, doors, and frames with precision, weatherproofing skill, and a strong focus on finish quality.

identity.title: Window Fitter
identity.summary: Fits and replaces windows, doors, and glazing units in homes and commercial buildings, ensuring accurate installation, insulation, and secure sealing.

salary.notes: Specialists in commercial fenestration, curtain walling, or bespoke installations can exceed £45k–£60k+.

qualifications: Apprenticeship or Level 2/3 Diploma in Fenestration Installation
Training in manual handling and working at height
Knowledge of frames, sealants, insulation, and glazing systems
Strong measurement accuracy and practical problem‑solving

lifestyle.description: Window fitters work on residential and commercial properties installing or replacing windows, doors, and glazing systems. Days involve removing old units, measuring openings, fitting new frames, sealing and insulating, and ensuring a clean, weatherproof finish. The role requires precision, physical strength, and customer‑facing professionalism. Work is often outdoors or in partially completed buildings. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy practical craftsmanship, problem‑solving, and producing clean, high‑quality results.

habits: Uses sealants and fixings cleanly and accurately
Follows safety procedures when working at height
